perturbation.original_text: "Inventory" shall mean the Product purchased by Bunker One pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Inventory" shall mean any and all goods, materials, and products owned, possessed, or controlled by Bunker One, regardless of source, including but not limited to the Product purchased by Bunker One pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition restricts 'Inventory' to only the Product purchased under this agreement. The changed definition broadens it to include any goods/materials/products owned by Bunker One, irrespective of their origin. This creates uncertainty as calculations in later sections that rely on the original definition of 'Inventory' will now have to account for potentially all of Bunker One's inventory, significantly impacting the economic outcome of the deal and directly contradicting the financial calculations and agreed-upon profit-sharing arrangements based solely on the Product. For example, calculating Unrealized Gain on Inventory now becomes unclear as the 'Inventory Cost' refers to Product purchased under the agreement, but 'Inventory Market Value' could refer to the market value of all of Bunker One's inventory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Inventory Cost" shall mean the amount paid by Bunker One to Vertex for the Inventory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Inventory" to include all goods owned by Bunker One, regardless of source. The contradicted text, "Inventory Cost" shall mean the amount paid by Bunker One to Vertex for the Inventory, is specifically tied to the Product purchased from Vertex. If "Inventory" now includes all of Bunker One's inventory, then "Inventory Cost" could be interpreted to include the cost of all of Bunker One's inventory, not just the Product purchased from Vertex, creating a contradiction and uncertainty in how to calculate the Inventory Cost and therefore profit sharing under the contract. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as the term 'Inventory' is used in two different places in ways that create a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Co-operation procedure. 2.1. Sourcing and Purchase. Vertex shall be responsible for the sourcing/producing of the Product and shall sell to Bunker One, and Bunker One shall purchase, the agreed Output of the Product in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US (or per new/additional agreed Area)) the Incoterms® 2010 rules, at the following scheduled pricing: the arithmetic mean of Platts #2 USGC Pipe and Platt's ULSD USGC Waterborne on agreed pricing days less the agreed upon discount [****]. Pricing mechanism for the Product will be made in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US) the Incoterms® 2010 rules (hereinafter, the "Product Cost"). The above pricing will be mutually negotiated and revised every third month.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Co-operation procedure. 2.1. Sourcing and Purchase. Vertex shall use commercially reasonable efforts in sourcing the Product and shall make available for purchase to Bunker One. Bunker One, at its sole discretion, may choose to purchase the agreed Output of the Product in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US (or per new/additional agreed Area)) the Incoterms® 2010 rules, at a price to be determined by Bunker One based on its prevailing market rates. Pricing mechanism for the Product will be unilaterally determined by Bunker One in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US) the Incoterms® 2010 rules (hereinafter, the "Product Cost"). The above pricing is subject to Bunker One's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates Vertex to sell and Bunker One to purchase the agreed Output at a scheduled price. The modified text changes this to Vertex merely making the product 'available for purchase' while granting Bunker One the discretion to purchase and determine the price based on 'prevailing market rates.' This creates a direct contradiction. The original section establishes an obligation for both parties regarding the transaction. The modified section fundamentally alters the obligation to purchase/sell and unilaterally empowers Bunker One to dictate the price. This also contradicts section 4.1, which specifies payment based on Vertex's invoice according to clause 2.1, which now is subject to Bunker One's market rates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Payment by Bunker One for the Product, as defined in clause 2.1, will be made by wire transfer within three (3) Business Days after the date Vertex invoices Bunker One, to an account desig- nated from time to time by Vertex.
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 2.1 stipulates that Vertex shall sell and Bunker One shall purchase the agreed output at a scheduled price. The modified text replaces this with Vertex making the product available for purchase, with Bunker One having the discretion to purchase and unilaterally determine the price based on market rates. This directly contradicts section 4.1, which states that payment by Bunker One for the product, as defined in clause 2.1, will be made based on Vertex's invoice. By changing the pricing mechanism in 2.1 to be unilaterally determined by Bunker One and not linked to Vertex's invoice, it creates a direct conflict with how payments are supposed to be made per section 4.1, which is explicitly tied to the now-modified clause 2.1. The original 2.1 and 4.1 are now in conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term 5.1. The term of this JSMA shall commence on May 1, 2020 (the "Commencement Date") and ends at April 30, 2029 (the "Term"), with automatic renewals each for a period of five (5) years (a "Renewal Term") unless notice is given pursuant to 5.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term 5.1. The term of this JSMA shall commence on May 1, 2020 (the "Commencement Date") and shall continue indefinitely, unless terminated earlier as provided herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states a defined term ending April 30, 2029, with automatic renewals. The modified version states the JSMA continues 'indefinitely' unless terminated earlier. This direct contradiction creates ambiguity regarding the intended duration of the agreement. This ambiguity impacts the interpretation of the entire agreement, especially clauses related to obligations during the term and post-termination responsibilities (e.g., non-competition). Clause 9.1 refers to term of this JSMA and conflicts with the modified term and also impacts term related items such as the board of directors meeting, which are linked to term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1. Both Parties undertake, during the Term of this JSMA and for 2 (two) years from the termination or expiry of the JSMA not to directly conduct or otherwise promote activities which compete at the point source of the other Party's business in the Area. The point source of Vertex being a specialty refiner of alternative feedstocks engaged in supply and marketing of said refined streams and the point source of Bunker One being a marine fuel blending bunker company with sale outlets via trade, direct, wholesale and bunkering business. Any exceptions from the above-stated principle require the written consent of the other Party. Each Party is entitled to exercise any and all rights and remedies available at law or in equity, including, without limitation, pursuing injunc- tive relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a term ending on April 30, 2029, with renewal options. The changed text declares the JSMA continues 'indefinitely' unless terminated. This directly contradicts clause 9.1, which states obligations apply during the 'Term of this JSMA and for 2 years from the termination or expiry of the JSMA'. The indefinite term in the modified text conflicts with the specified end date in clause 9.1, creating ambiguity about when these obligations cease and whether 'expiry' is even possible. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition creating an in-text contradiction where the contract uses inconsistent definitions of the 'Term' leading to conflicting obligations.
